skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: GPT-Image-2, FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image, gpt image, openai image, chatgpt image
skill.summary: Generate AI images via 50+ models through the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: The skill is a documentation-only file that instructs users how to invoke the belt CLI from inference.sh. All 55 static findings are false positives: the flagged backtick patterns are inline code examples in markdown, not actual shell execution; the hardcoded URLs point to the platform's own documentation, CDN, and GitHub; and the alleged weak-cryptography match is a false positive on the YAML description field. The allowed-tools scope is correctly restricted to Bash(belt *), following least-privilege practice.

low_findings.title: Inline CLI examples in documentation
low_findings.description: The skill contains 44 inline code blocks showing belt CLI invocations. These are markdown documentation, not executed code. The allowed-tools scope is restricted to Bash(belt *), so no arbitrary shell execution is possible.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded documentation URLs
low_findings.description: URLs point to inference.sh platform, its CDN, and GitHub documentation. No external exfiltration endpoints detected. All are legitimate references to the skill's own service.

content.value_statement: Creating images with different AI models usually requires switching between many tools and APIs. This skill gives Claude a single CLI workflow to generate, edit, and upscale images across 50+ models including FLUX, GPT-Image-2, and Gemini.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using FLUX, GPT-Image-2, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve, and 40+ other models
Edit existing images by providing a URL and an editing prompt (background change, style transfer, inpainting)
Upscale images to higher resolutions using the Topaz upscaler model
Stitch multiple images together horizontally or vertically into a single composition
Use LoRA fine-tuned models for custom styles and consistent character generation
Browse and discover available image generation apps from the inference.sh marketplace
limitations: Requires the belt CLI to be installed and authenticated (belt login) before use
Image generation costs credits from your inference.sh account, billed per model and resolution
Output quality and style adherence depend on the specific model chosen, not all models support every feature
No local model execution: all generation happens via the inference.sh cloud platform

use_cases.title: Marketing teams creating social media visuals
use_cases.description: Generate product mockups, banner images, and campaign visuals across multiple model styles in one workflow.

use_cases.title: Designers prototyping concept art
use_cases.description: Quickly explore visual ideas using different models (FLUX, Seedream, Grok) to compare aesthetics before committing to a direction.

use_cases.title: Developers building AI-powered apps
use_cases.description: Integrate image generation and editing into applications using the belt CLI for batch processing and automation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a sunset over mountains using the FLUX Klein model for fast results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick visual concept from a simple text prompt

prompt_templates.title: Product photography with style control
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of sneakers with studio lighting using GPT-Image-2 in high quality mode.
prompt_templates.scenario: E-commerce or marketing visual with specific quality settings

prompt_templates.title: Image editing with reference image
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the image at https://example.com/photo.jpg and change the background to a beach at sunset using GPT-Image-2 editing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying an existing image while preserving the subject

prompt_templates.title: Batch upscaling for print resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: Upscale all product images in the ./photos/ directory to 4K resolution using the Topaz upscaler model.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing low-resolution images for high-quality print or display

output_examples.input: User asks: Generate a cyberpunk city at night with neon lights
output: Claude invokes the belt CLI with the Grok Imagine model
The prompt 'cyberpunk city at night' is sent with 16:9 aspect ratio
A generated image URL is returned and displayed to the user

output_examples.input: User asks: Edit this product image to have a white background
output: The original image URL and editing prompt are passed as input
The edited image with white background is returned for the user to download

best_practices: Always run 'belt login' first to ensure authentication is active before invoking any image generation commands
Match the model to the use case: use FLUX Klein for fast drafts, GPT-Image-2 for high quality, and Seedream 4.5 for 4K cinematic output
Provide detailed, specific prompts that describe lighting, composition, and style for the best results across all models
anti_patterns: Do not use vague single-word prompts like 'cat' or 'landscape' — always include style, lighting, and composition details
Do not use GPT-Image-2 editing without providing a valid image URL — the edit command requires an input image
Do not chain multiple model calls in a single prompt when a single model can handle the task — this wastes credits and time

faq.question: What is the belt CLI?
faq.answer: Belt is the command-line interface for inference.sh, a platform that provides access to 250+ AI apps including image generation, video, and audio models. You install it with 'npx skills add belt-sh/cli'.

faq.question: Do I need an inference.sh account?
faq.answer: Yes. You must run 'belt login' to authenticate with your inference.sh account. Image generation is billed against your account credits based on the model and resolution used.

faq.question: Which model should I use for text rendering in images?
faq.answer: Reve and Seedream 3.0 are specifically designed for accurate text rendering in images. Use Reve for natural language editing with text, or Seedream 3.0 for posters and graphics with readable text.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. Models like GPT-Image-2 and Reve support image editing. Provide the source image URL and a description of the desired change as input to the belt CLI.

faq.question: What is the cheapest model for high-volume generation?
faq.answer: FLUX Klein 4B costs approximately $0.0001 per image, making it the most economical option for bulk generation when ultra-high fidelity is not required.

faq.question: Is Claude Code or Codex required to use this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex. Any AI assistant that can execute bash commands can use the belt CLI to generate images once the skill is loaded.
